shower.thought: You'll never know if you're remembering something accurately or just remembering the last time you remembered it.
The people who need therapy the most are often the least likely to go, which means the system is backwards.
You spend your whole life learning how to be an adult, and just when you figure it out, your body starts betraying you.
Nostalgia is just your brain editing out all the parts that actually sucked.
Everyone's a hypocrite about something—the only difference is which thing.
You can't actually prove anyone else is conscious, so technically you're surrounded by philosophical zombies who might actually be real.
The worse you are at something, the more confident you tend to be about it.
Most of your personality is just trauma responses that eventually got labeled "quirks."
Your parents did the best they could with the mental health awareness they had, which is both comforting and devastating.
Boredom might be the feeling of your potential energy converting to regret.
We call it "self-care" now, but it used to just be called "not destroying yourself."
The saddest people you know probably make the best jokes because they've had the most practice.
You're probably only one or two bad decisions away from being a completely different person.
Silence used to be free, and now we pay for it with noise-canceling headphones.
The internet has made it possible to be perfectly, precisely yourself to thousands of strangers while being completely fake to the people who know you best.
Your childhood self would be disappointed in you, which is why you try not to think about it.
The only way to truly know yourself is to be observed by someone who loves you and is willing to be honest.
Most advice is just someone else's autobiography dressed up as wisdom.
You've probably had thousands of conversations that changed someone else's life without knowing it.
Everyone's doing their own version of pretending they have it together, which means nobody actually does.

motivational.thought:
Progress is measured in small victories, not perfect moments.
— Unknown

The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
— Confucius

Your past doesn't determine your future; your effort does.
— Unknown

Regret for the things we did is temporary; regret for the things we didn't do is permanent.
— Sydney Harris

The only way out is through.
— Robert Frost

Excellence is not a destination; it's a habit.
— Aristotle

You cannot control the outcome, only the effort. Make the effort count.
— Unknown

What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
— Ralph Waldo Emerson

The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it.
— Chinese Proverb

Comfort is the enemy of growth.
— Unknown

Every master was once a beginner who refused to give up.
— Unknown

The obstacle is the way.
— Marcus Aurelius

Your only competition is who you were yesterday.
— Unknown

Consistency beats intensity. Show up when it's boring, when it's hard, when no one is watching.
— Unknown

A smooth sea never made a skilled sailor.
— Unknown

Success is not final; failure is not fatal. It is the courage to continue that counts.
— Winston Churchill

You earn respect by attempting difficult things, not by avoiding them.
— Unknown

The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
— Chinese Proverb

Don't wait for permission to build the life you want.
— Unknown

Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
— Jim Rohn

darkjoke.thought:
I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high.
She looked surprised.

My grandfather said, "Your generation is too reliant on technology."
I said, "You're right," and unplugged his life support.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.

My dad always said I'd never amount to anything.
He was right, but I didn't think he'd live long enough to gloat about it.

I went to a wedding where the bride was absolutely stunning.
The groom looked like he was already regretting his life choices, which, to be fair, was just the first of many.

My therapist asked what my biggest fear is.
I said, "Honest feedback."
She's been quiet ever since.

I'm reading a book on the history of glue.
I can't put it down.

My friend asked if I wanted to hear a joke about construction.
I said yes, but he's still working on it.
He's been working on it for six months.
I think he might be avoiding me.

My mother-in-law asked how many drinks I'd had.
I said, "Why, are you counting?"
She said, "Yes, for the eulogy."

I told my kid that Santa isn't real.
She cried for an hour.
Then I told her about her college fund and she felt better.

My coworker said he had a great weekend.
I asked what he did.
He said he finally cleaned out his garage.
I realized then that his life and my life were very different.

I asked my friend why he was crying at the gym.
He said, "These are just sweat tears."
We both knew that wasn't true.
We both knew it was the divorce papers.

My doctor said I needed to cut back on salt.
I said, "What if I quit my family instead?"
He said that would probably help more.

I went to a museum and there was a sign that said "Please Do Not Touch the Exhibits."
I respected that.
Unlike my father, who didn't respect a lot of boundaries.

My girlfriend asked if I'd ever been in love before.
I said, "Yes, once."
She looked devastated.
I said, "Don't worry, it was a long time ago."
She asked when.
I said, "Yesterday, but it passed."

My boss asked why I looked so sad.
I said my dog died.
He said, "Oh no, how old was he?"
I said, "I don't know, he didn't have a birthday cake."
My boss just stared at me.
I think I'm getting fired.

I love my country.
It's the people in it that I can't stand.

My therapist said I was in denial.
I said I wasn't.
She said, "Exactly."

I asked the pharmacist if there was anything to help with my anxiety.
She said, "Yes, stop coming in here."

My friend said he was going to therapy to work through his childhood trauma.
I said, "Good luck, that stuff never goes away."
He said, "How do you know?"
I said, "Because you're still hanging around me."
